# Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct

## Our pledge

This project and everyone participating in it is governed by this Code of Conduct. We're building Mendel to be a genuinely open, broadly contributable project attacking a hard technical problem — that only works if participation is welcoming and harassment-free regardless of age, body size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

## Expected behavior

- Be respectful of differing viewpoints and experiences — this project moves fast on unsettled technical questions, and disagreement on approach is normal
- Give and receive constructive feedback gracefully; focus criticism on ideas and code, not people
- Assume good faith, especially in text-only communication where tone is easy to misread

## Unacceptable behavior

- Harassment, insults, or derogatory comments, public or private
- Personal or political attacks unrelated to the technical discussion at hand
- Publishing others' private information without explicit permission
- Sustained disruption of issues, PRs, or discussions
- Any other conduct reasonably considered inappropriate in a professional setting

## Scope

Applies within all project spaces (issues, PRs, Discussions, and any official Mendel channels) and in public spaces when someone is representing the project.

## Enforcement

Report unacceptable behavior to the maintainers by opening a private security advisory on GitHub, or by contacting **@ichidera** directly via GitHub. Reports are handled promptly and in confidence. Maintainers who violate this Code of Conduct are held to the same standard as any other contributor.

Consequences are decided case by case under the project's BDFL governance model (see `GOVERNANCE.md`) and may range from a private warning to a permanent ban from project spaces, depending on severity and pattern.

## Attribution

Adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/), version 2.1, for Mendel's scale and governance model.
